DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Cesar R. Fumar of the law offices of Cesar R. Fumar (925-757-9800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Karl S. Kronenberger of the law offices of Kronenberger Rosenfeld (415-955-1155) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KAI TAM and GISU KIM's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jack Chow is off calendar as to Ms. Tam and overruled as to Mr. Kim on both causes of action. Ms. Tam's demurrer is off calendar in light of the ruling granting her anti-SLAPP motion to strike as to both causes of action alleged against her. Mr. Kim's demurrer is overruled because, as implied in the ruling on the anti-SLAPP motion, Dr. Chow has adequately alleged that some of the statements in the YELP post were false and unprivileged, Mr. Kim committed outrageous conduct against Dr. Chow, and that outrageous conduct resulted in Dr. Chow suffering severe emotional distress. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com. (L&M302)

Appearances:
Attorney, Cesar R. Fumar of the law offices of Cesar R. Fumar (925-757-9800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Karl S. Kronenberger of the law offices of Kronenberger Rosenfeld (415-955-1155) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KAI TAM and GISU KIM's MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jack Chow is granted as to both causes of action alleged against Ms. Tam and denied as to both causes of action alleged against Mr. Kim. Both plaintiffs satisfied their first prong burden of showing that both causes of action alleged against them are based on activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Kim's Yelp post is protected activity per CCP 425.16(e)(3) because it is a writing made in a public forum regarding consumer safety and asserted substandard dental care, which are issues of public interest. Mr. Kim's telephone calls and the statements he made at the courthouse are protected activity per CCP 425.16(e)(2) because they are oral statements made in connection with contemplated or actual litigation. Dr. Chow failed to satisfy his second prong burden as to Ms. Tam because he provided no evidence showing that Ms. Tam was the agent of Mr. Kim with respect to the YELP post, Mr. Kim's telephone calls or the courthouse statements. Dr. Chow satisfied his second prong burden as to Mr. Kim of providing evidence showing that there is minimal merit to both of his causes of action alleged against Mr. Kim. As to the first cause of action for libel, a reasonable trier of fact could find based on the evidence submitted on this motion that the statements in the YELP post that the "crown ' broke twice in a course of a few months," "he refused to pay the other dentist for the work done," and "His receptionist threatens us that he will not pay" were false and made by Mr. Kim with the knowledge that they were false or with reckless disregard of whether they were false or not. As to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, a reasonable trier of fact could find based on the evidence submitted on this motion that Mr. Kim acted outrageously by lying about Dr. Chow in the YELP post, misrepresenting himself as an attorney, and making extortionate threats to report Dr. Chow to an insurer, the Dental Board and the business bureaus and file a malpractice case against Dr. Chow if he didn't pay Dr. Lee's bill. On the IIED cause of action, a reasonable trier of fact could also find that Dr. Chow suffered severe emotional distress as a result of Mr. Kim's outrageous conduct. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com. (L&M302)
